\"interpretation\": \"MU trades at SIGNIFICANT DISCOUNT to memory peers on forward earnings/EBITDA — the classic 'memory cycle peak' valuation pattern: when the cycle peaks, P/E and EV/EBITDA optically look 'cheap' because earnings have spiked and the market is already pricing future cyclical compression. The 7.8× FY2 P/E is consistent with 'late-cycle' memory pricing. Cycle-aware investors do NOT mechanically interpret this as cheap — they understand that FY2 earnings could compress materially if the HBM cycle moderates. The +83% P/Sales premium reflects market expectation of structural HBM moat. Sell-side target $588.59 sits BELOW current $746.81 (-21%), implying analysts haven't fully captured the recent rally OR are pricing in normalization. Importantly, the FactSet 'Semiconductor Memory' peer set is heavily Asian (SK Hynix, Nanya, etc.); for US-listed memory peer comparison, Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate (STX) would also be relevant but are not in the FactSet memory peer set.\", \"live_price_during_capture\": 781.12, \"live_quote_date\": \"2026-05-11 11:22 Exch Time\", \"source_notes\": \"Captured 2026-05-11 via FactSet Workstation Web Comps Analysis (Company/Security → Comps Analysis → Semiconductor Memory peer set, All Regions, mkt cap $10B-$900B custom range).
